2603.29723.title: Reinforced Reasoning for End-to-End Retrosynthetic Planning
2603.29723.paper_summary: Retrosynthetic planning is a fundamental task in organic chemistry, yet remains challenging due to its combinatorial complexity. To address this, conventional approaches typically rely on hybrid frameworks that combine single-step predictions with external search heuristics, inevitably fracturing the logical coherence between local molecular transformations and global planning objectives. To bridge this gap and embed sophisticated strategic foresight directly into the model's chemical reasoning, we introduce ReTriP, an end-to-end generative framework that reformulates retrosynthesis as a direct Chain-of-Thought reasoning task. We establish a path-coherent molecular representation and employ a progressive training curriculum that transitions from reasoning distillation to reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, effectively aligning stepwise generation with practical route utility. Empirical evaluation on RetroBench demonstrates that ReTriP achieves state-of-the-art performance, exhibiting superior robustness in long-horizon planning compared to hybrid baselines.
2603.29723.paper_summary_zh: 逆合成規劃是有機化學中的一項基本任務，但由於其組合複雜性，仍然具有挑戰性。為了解決這個問題，傳統方法通常依賴混合框架，將單步預測與外部搜索啟發式結合，這不可避免地破壞了局部分子轉換與全球規劃目標之間的邏輯一致性。為了彌補這一差距並將複雜的戰略前瞻性直接嵌入模型的化學推理中，我們引入了ReTriP，一個端到端的生成框架，將逆合成重新定義為直接的思維鏈推理任務。我們建立了一種路徑一致的分子表示，並採用逐步的訓練課程，從推理蒸餾過渡到具有可驗證獎勵的強化學習，有效地將逐步生成與實際路徑效用對齊。在RetroBench上的實證評估顯示，ReTriP達到了最先進的性能，在長期規劃中表現出比混合基準更優越的穩健性。
2603.29723.author: Chenyang Zuo et.al.
2603.29723.authors: Chenyang Zuo, Siqi Fan, Yizhen Luo, Zaiqing Nie
